rationale: The citing paper’s stated research gap and question are explicitly about adding an effect “beyond traditional mere measurement,” and it positions itself as extending mere measurement/question-behavior work. The cited paper is a core mere measurement study that develops mechanism and boundary conditions (response fluency, transfer-appropriate processing), making it a plausible theoretical foundation rather than an incidental mention; the pair is also directly DOI-matched (10.1509/jmkr.44.2.309).
